data.title: Fed Chair Powell Highlights Differential Impact of High Oil Prices on U.S. Economy
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated that high oil prices have a lesser impact on the U.S. economy compared to Europe and Asia. According to NS3.AI, Powell noted that the U.S. would experience a more significant effect if elevated oil prices continue over a prolonged period.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC
data.description: According to Whale Alert, a significant transaction occurred with the minting of 250 million USDC at the USDC Treasury. This development highlights ongoing activities within the stablecoin market, reflecting the demand and liquidity requirements in the cryptocurrency ecosystem. The minting of such a large amount of USDC could have implications for market dynamics and investor strategies.

data.title: Jim Cramer Criticizes Robinhood's Move into Prediction Markets
data.description: Jim Cramer has expressed concerns over Robinhood's decision to enter the prediction market sector, suggesting it may bolster its image as a platform focused on speculation. According to NS3.AI, Cramer highlighted the prevalence of fraudulent activities within the prediction market space, warning that Robinhood's involvement could expose users to potential scams.

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices surged intraday, with WTI rising 8.48%.
data.description: Gate data shows that Brent crude oil rose above $111 per barrel, up 6.82% on the day. WTI crude oil rose above $110 per barrel, up 8.48% on the day. Iran stated that revenge for its assassinated leader remains on the agenda.

data.title: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Powell: Tariff Inflation Expected to Ease This Year
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has expressed his belief that tariff-induced inflation should ease within this year. According to Jin10, Powell anticipates that the reduction in inflationary pressures from tariffs will begin soon. His comments come amid ongoing discussions about the impact of tariffs on the U.S. economy and inflation rates.

data.title: Meta Platforms Plans to Extend Server Operation Amid Memory Shortage
data.description: Meta Platforms (META.O) is planning to extend the operational lifespan of some of its servers during a period of memory shortage. According to Jin10, this decision comes as the company faces challenges in acquiring sufficient memory resources to maintain its usual server operations. The move is aimed at optimizing resource usage and ensuring continued service delivery despite the constraints. Meta is exploring various strategies to mitigate the impact of the shortage and maintain its technological infrastructure.

data.title: Powell: Policy is likely to remain in a neutral range
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell stated at a press conference that monetary policy is likely within a neutral range in terms of its impact on the economy. He said, "I think we are very close to the neutral rate, which is probably in the range of 3% to 4%, while the current target range for the federal funds rate is 3.5% to 3.75%." He added, "If we need to raise rates, we will signal it and raise rates, and vice versa." (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Federal Reserve Chairman Powell Predicts Tariff Effects in Upcoming Quarters
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell has indicated that the impact of tariffs is expected to become apparent within the next two quarters. According to NS3.AI, Powell's statement highlights the anticipated economic consequences of trade policies, which could influence market dynamics and investor sentiment. The Federal Reserve continues to monitor these developments closely as they assess potential implications for monetary policy and economic growth.

data.title: Meta Introduces Stablecoin Payouts for Creators on Solana and Polygon
data.description: Meta, serving over 3 billion users globally, has initiated stablecoin payouts in Circle's USDC for a select group of creators on Solana and Polygon. According to NS3.AI, this feature is accessible to eligible creators in Colombia and the Philippines, allowing them to link a crypto wallet to receive earnings. Stripe facilitates the service with crypto-related reporting, and creators might receive tax documents from both Meta and Stripe related to payouts and digital asset transactions.

data.title: Federal Reserve Members See Balanced Odds for Rate Changes
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has indicated that more committee members are now viewing the likelihood of a rate hike and a rate cut as similar. According to NS3.AI, Powell emphasized that currently, there is no advocacy for an interest rate increase among the members.

data.title: Powell: Determined to defend the Fed's independence, will continue serving as a Fed governor "for some time".
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell stated at a press conference that he will continue to serve as a Federal Reserve Governor "for some time" after stepping down as chairman. Powell pointed out that the Fed has suffered unprecedented setbacks over the past three months, and he is determined to defend the Fed's independence. Powell clarified that the setbacks he referred to were not political criticism, but merely legal proceedings. He said, "What has happened over the past three months has left me with no choice but to remain in office." Powell stated that the Department of Justice will not reopen its investigation against him unless the Inspector General files a criminal referral. However, Powell was not satisfied with this, saying, "I will continue to serve as a Federal Reserve Governor, pending further notice," and promised to "maintain a low profile." (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: Unexpected divisions within the Fed; Warsh faces numerous challenges upon taking office.
data.description: Analysts Anna and Stuart pointed out that today's unchanged interest rate policy was expected, but the dissenting opinions were noteworthy. Ironically, Powell, widely seen as a consensus-builder—and likely at his last meeting as Fed chair—led the meeting with the most dissenting voices. The policy statement also upgraded the description of inflation from "somewhat high" to "high." Coupled with the apparent divisions within the committee, this highlights the challenges Kevin Warsh faces in achieving Trump's desired rate cut target. Unless there is a significant deterioration in the labor market, it's hard to imagine this divided committee acting quickly to cut rates. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Denies Being a Shadow Leader
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated that he would never become a shadow leader. According to Odaily, Powell made this remark in response to concerns about his leadership style and influence within the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell: Labor Market Not a Source of Inflation
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated that he does not view the labor market as a source of inflation. According to Jin10, Powell emphasized that the current inflationary pressures are not primarily driven by labor market conditions. He noted that other factors are contributing to inflation, and the Fed is closely monitoring these developments to guide its policy decisions. Powell's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the impact of wage growth and employment levels on inflation rates. The Federal Reserve continues to assess economic indicators to determine the appropriate course of action for monetary policy.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Highlights Increased Tensions Since March
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has noted that the current situation is more tense compared to March. According to ChainCatcher, Powell's remarks underscore the evolving economic challenges faced by the U.S. Federal Reserve. The statement reflects ongoing concerns about financial stability and market conditions.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Considers Energy and Tariff Factors Before Rate Cuts
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has indicated that before contemplating any reduction in interest rates, it is essential to evaluate the implications of energy and tariffs. According to ChainCatcher, Powell emphasized the importance of understanding these factors thoroughly as they play a significant role in economic decision-making. His remarks highlight the cautious approach the Federal Reserve is taking in response to current economic conditions.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: I will never become a shadow chairman.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: I will never be a shadow chairman. (Jinshi)

data.title: Rwa Wealth Partners CIO Comments on Unprecedented Opposition
data.description: On April 30, Jin10 reported that JP Powers, Chief Investment Officer at Rwa Wealth Partners, remarked on the unusual level of opposition seen for the first time in over 30 years, calling it a 'crazy time.' According to Jin10, Powers noted that Milan is somewhat unconventional, advocating for further interest rate cuts at this moment. He expressed certainty that reaching any consensus within the committee would be challenging, especially with oil prices back above $100. Additionally, the transition involving Powell adds difficulty to advancing such measures. Interestingly, Powers highlighted that three other committee members were unwilling to maintain a dovish stance in their statement, which he believes is the crux of the matter.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's Confirmation Hearing Highlights Internal Fed Conflict
data.description: Nick Timiraos has suggested that four dissenting votes within the Federal Reserve may bolster Kevin Warsh's assertion of internal discord. According to NS3.AI, this observation was made in relation to Warsh's confirmation hearing held last week.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Comments on Duration of Ukraine and Iran Conflicts
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump remarked that the conflicts in Ukraine and Iran have persisted for comparable lengths of time. According to NS3.AI, he suggested that these wars might reach their conclusions simultaneously.
